mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals stronger near-term household spending and revenue expectations; higher RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of observations, or if the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above a tightening threshold and continues rising, higher discount rates reduce equity valuation multiples; the signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0% and its 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations, or if triggered negative-return hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion combined with falling unemployment signals broad labor-income growth without rising joblessness; joint strength in PAYEMS and UNRATE predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down signal does not outperform either single-series signal, or if triggered 20-day returns are positive in less than 55% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: High CPI inflation while FEDFUNDS remains near zero signals negative real policy rates and future policy-tightening risk; this interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month inflation exceeds 3.0% while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the high-CPI and low-FEDFUNDS interaction has a nonnegative average 60-day forward return, or if its downside hit rate is below 55% after transaction-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving physical output and operating leverage for cyclical earnings; higher INDPRO momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is followed by negative or zero average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if its walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and durable-goods demand; when HOUST falls below the threshold, it predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1000 triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth with elevated unemployment signals an early-cycle expansion with slack, allowing earnings recovery without immediate wage-pressure tightening; the interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth is positive and UNRATE is above 5.5%
failure_condition: Reject if this GDPC1-positive and UNRATE-elevated state fails to deliver higher average 60-day returns than other expansion observations, or if positive-return hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Very low FEDFUNDS suppresses cash yields and supports near-term equity risk-taking; when policy rates remain near the zero lower bound, the signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS-below-0.25% observations have a lower mean 5-day S&P 500 return than observations with higher FEDFUNDS, or if the positive-return hit rate is below 50%.
